function_explanation: Performs a centered 2D Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) to convert an image from spatial domain to k-space (frequency domain).
function_docstring:
Performs a centered 2D Fast Fourier Transform on an input image.

This function converts an image from the spatial domain to k-space (frequency
domain) using a centered FFT. The centering is achieved by applying ifftshift
before the FFT and fftshift after, which moves the zero-frequency component
to the center of the spectrum.

Args:
    img: A 2D numpy array representing the input image in spatial domain.
        Can be real or complex valued with shape (H, W) where H is height
        and W is width.

Returns:
    A complex numpy array of the same shape as the input, representing
    the centered k-space (frequency domain) data.
usage_example:
import numpy as np

# Create a sample 2D image
image = np.random.randn(128, 128)

# Transform to centered k-space
kspace = fft2c(image)

# kspace is now a complex array with zero-frequency at center
print(kspace.shape)  # (128, 128)
print(kspace.dtype)  # complex128
